abstract: PHP 8.x best practices guide for AI agents and LLMs. Contains 56 rules across 9 sections (types, modern PHP, error handling, security, performance, SOLID/design, PSR/structure, tooling/static analysis, legacy migration). Emphasises strict typing, native types first, PHPDoc as a precision layer only when native PHP cannot express enough, value objects over primitives, no magic-heavy design, analyzable code, and explicit error handling. Includes first-principles Problem Definition, RCA workflow, Kanban phases, and mandatory PHPStan + php-cs-fixer validation loop. Always detect the project's PHP version before suggesting features.

categories.name: Types
categories.description: declare(strict_types=1), parameter types, return types, property types, nullable discipline, union/intersection types, void/never, avoiding mixed

categories.name: Modern PHP
categories.description: Constructor promotion (8.0+), enums (8.1+), readonly (8.1+/8.2+), typed constants and #[Override] (8.3+), property hooks and asymmetric visibility (8.4+), pipe operator (8.5+, deployment-dependent)

categories.name: Error Handling
categories.description: Catch specific exceptions, custom exceptions when callers react differently, shallow exception hierarchies, finally for cleanup only, never suppress errors

categories.name: Security
categories.description: Input validation at boundaries, context-aware output escaping, prepared statements always, password_hash/verify, file upload validation

categories.name: Performance
categories.description: Native string functions over regex, native array functions with readability first, generators for streaming, lazy loading for genuinely expensive deps, no globals

categories.name: SOLID / Design
categories.description: SRP, OCP, LSP, ISP, DIP — applied pragmatically. Value objects over primitives (design-value-objects). No magic-heavy design — prefer explicit typed methods over __get/__call (design-no-magic).

categories.name: PSR / Structure
categories.description: PSR-4 autoloading, PSR-12 style via tooling, file structure discipline, domain-reflecting namespaces, intent-revealing class and method names

categories.name: Tooling / Static Analysis
categories.description: PHPStan + php-cs-fixer before every commit, enforced in CI (tooling-phpstan-cs-fixer). PHPDoc precision layer — @template, array shapes, class-string, int-ranges only when native types cannot express enough (phpstan-phpdoc). Inline @var is a last resort.

categories.name: Legacy Migration
categories.description: Incremental modernisation: PHPStan baseline → Rector syntax transforms → type coverage file-by-file → Strangler Fig isolation of legacy modules. Never rewrite everything at once.

keyFeatures: First-principles Problem Definition checklist before coding
RCA (Root Cause Analysis) workflow for bugs and quality violations
Version detection: always check composer.json before suggesting features
9-section structure ordered by impact: Types first, then Modern PHP, Error Handling, Security, Performance, SOLID/Design, PSR/Structure, Tooling, Legacy Migration
56 rules using Bad / Better / Best three-tier examples
final and readonly by default where applicable
Native types first; PHPDoc only when native PHP cannot express enough
Pipe operator (8.5+) explicitly marked as deployment-dependent
Property hooks (8.4+) and asymmetric visibility (8.4+) accurately version-gated
Value objects over primitives: self-validating, immutable domain types (design-value-objects)
No-magic-heavy design: explicit typed methods over __get/__call (design-no-magic)
PHPStan-style PHPDoc: generics, array shapes, class-string, int-ranges (phpstan-phpdoc)
Legacy migration: PHPStan baseline, Rector transforms, Strangler Fig pattern (legacy-migration)
Mandatory PHPStan + php-cs-fixer validation loop with CI integration (tooling-phpstan-cs-fixer)
Response Template for structured PHP task responses
